mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 7.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in more than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested against 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns as growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but S&P 500 5-day returns remain negative in over 65% of observations during easing periods, or the signal reverses direction during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC against 20-day forward returns over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index rises above 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals a structural shift in demand composition, where weak consumption relative to GDP forecasts earnings misses and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns in over 55% of cases, or the combined signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment and rise in payroll employment during a supply-constrained regime indicates labor market tightness that triggers wage-push inflation fears, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations during supply-constrained periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.
